title: gentiana triflora subvar. montana
intro: Gentiana triflora subvar. montana, also known as the Gentian Bitter, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Europe. It is a member of the Gentianaceae family and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its bitter compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and improve liver function. The health benefits of Gentian Bitter include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and indigestion, and support the immune system. Gentian Bitter can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
